# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 157

This is turn 157. Turns 1 through 156 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 157.

According to the attached 2026-06-23 history, Turn 156 left the frozen 256-byte \(h_0\) submission unchanged and closed the Proposition-II provenance gap. 

# Turn 157 — target-side terminal/active-block audit

The submission remains:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
g0 = sigma2^2
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0
dg = d0^g0
hcomm = dg^-1*d0^-1*dg*d0
h0 = (x0^g0)*x0*dg*d0*d0^2*hcomm

h0*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

I independently reproduced its serialization:

\[
\text{size}=256,\qquad
\operatorname{SHA256}
=\mathtt{4f81b04ae00e737a244f2445d70b095333a427508958f11489e3cabdc0192fdc}.
\]

The tame relation remains implicit, and the exponent denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its required verifier residue. 

The source-sensitive interfaces—boundary, elementary deformation duality, and determinant sign—now have executable certificates. The complementary load-bearing issue is the purely target-side theorem that every nonterminal marked target admits the asserted active block and that every recurrence child has strictly smaller marked \(2\)-kernel. I audited that argument from its hypotheses rather than checking another finite group.

## 1. Terminal targets are exactly tame–\(2\)-group pullbacks

Let \(Y\) be a finite framed target, let \(L\triangleleft Y\) be its marked normal \(2\)-subgroup, and put

\[
H=Y/L.
\]

Assume every \(Y\)-chief factor contained in \(L\) is trivial. Let \(H_2\) be the maximal \(2\)-quotient of \(H\), and let

\[
N=\ker(H\to H_2).
\]

Because \(H\) is a finite tame quotient, \(N\) has odd order. Let \(P\) be its inverse image in \(Y\), and choose a Schur–Zassenhaus complement \(\widetilde N\leq P\).

Take a \(Y\)-chief series

\[
1=L_0\triangleleft L_1\triangleleft\cdots\triangleleft L_m=L.
\]

Triviality of every factor gives

\[
[L_i,\widetilde N]\leq L_{i-1}.
\]

Hence \([L,{}_m\widetilde N]=1\). Coprime action gives

\[
[L,\widetilde N]=[L,\widetilde N,\widetilde N]
=\cdots=[L,{}_m\widetilde N]=1.
\]

Thus \(P=L\times\widetilde N\). All complements are \(L\)-conjugate, but \(L\) centralizes \(\widetilde N\), so the complement is unique. Consequently \(\widetilde N\triangleleft Y\).

Setting \(Q=Y/\widetilde N\), the group \(Q\) is a \(2\)-group and the two quotient maps give an isomorphism

\[
Y\;\cong\;H\times_{H_2}Q. \tag{157.1}
\]

Moreover, projection to \(Q\) induces an exact image-poset correspondence

\[
\left\{J\leq Y:\operatorname{pr}_H(J)=H\right\}
\longleftrightarrow
\left\{Q'\leq Q:Q'\twoheadrightarrow H_2\right\},
\]

with \(J=H\times_{H_2}Q'\). The proof is the coprime-kernel subdirect lemma: the two kernels have odd and \(2\)-power orders, so a subgroup surjecting onto both factors is the full pullback.

Thus the terminal branch is not merely count-equivalent to a \(2\)-group problem. Its entire exact-image stratification is literally the compatible-subgroup stratification of \(Q\), evaluated using the common marked maximal pro-\(2\) boundary. This confirms the terminal classification recorded earlier. 

## 2. Every nonterminal target has the required active block

Suppose the terminal condition fails. Choose

\[
S\triangleleft P\triangleleft Y,\qquad P/S\cong V,
\]

where all chief factors below \(S\) are trivial and \(V\) is the first nontrivial simple \(\mathbf F_2[Y]\)-module. Choose \(K\triangleleft Y\), minimal subject to

\[
K\leq P,\qquad KS=P.
\]

Put

\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad
M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=(K\cap S)/R,\qquad
C=Y/K.
\]

I obtain the complete active-block structure

\[
R\leq Z(K),\qquad K^4=1, \tag{157.2}
\]

\[
R\ \text{elementary abelian},\qquad
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}(M), \tag{157.3}
\]

\[
M/T_0\cong V,\qquad (M^\vee)^C=0. \tag{157.4}
\]

### Centrality of \(R\)

Let \(\widetilde N\) be an odd Hall lift of the kernel of \(H\to H_2\). The same scalar-chief-series argument gives

\[
[S,\widetilde N]=1.
\]

Since \(K/(K\cap S)\cong V\) is elementary abelian,

\[
R=\Phi(K)\leq K\cap S,
\]

so \(\widetilde N\) centralizes \(R\).

Now \(C_K(R)\) is characteristic in \(K\), hence normal in \(Y\). The three-subgroup lemma gives

\[
[K,\widetilde N]\leq C_K(R).
\]

The normal \(2\)-subgroup \(L\) acts trivially on the simple characteristic-two module \(V\). If \(N\) also acted trivially, the whole action would factor through the \(2\)-group \(H/N\), forcing \(V\) to be trivial. Therefore \(N\) acts nontrivially. Since \(N\triangleleft H\) and \(V\) is simple,

\[
[V,N]=V.
\]

Consequently

\[
[K,\widetilde N]S=P.
\]

It follows that \(C_K(R)S=P\). Minimality of \(K\) forces \(C_K(R)=K\), proving \(R\leq Z(K)\).

### Exponent four

Because \(K/R\) is elementary abelian, \(x^2\in R\leq Z(K)\). Thus

\[
[x,y]^2=[x^2,y]=1.
\]

In this class-two group the map

\[
f:K\to R,\qquad f(x)=x^4
\]

is a homomorphism and is \(Y\)-equivariant. Since \(\widetilde N\) fixes \(R\),

\[
f([k,n])=f(k)^{-1}f(k^n)=1.
\]

Let \(J\) be the \(Y\)-normal closure of \([K,\widetilde N]\). Its image modulo \(S\) is all of \(V\), so \(JS=P\). Minimality again gives \(J=K\). Hence \(f=1\) on \(K\), proving \(K^4=1\).

Therefore

\[
R=\Phi(K)=K^2[K,K]
\]

is generated by central involutions and is elementary abelian.

### The scalar radical is exactly \(T_0\)

Let \(U<M\) be a proper \(C\)-submodule. If \(U\nsubseteq T_0\), its image in the simple quotient \(M/T_0\cong V\) is nonzero and hence surjective. The inverse image \(\widehat U\triangleleft Y\) then satisfies

\[
\widehat U S=P,
\]

contradicting the minimality of \(K\). Thus every proper submodule lies in \(T_0\), making \(T_0\) the unique maximal submodule:

\[
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_C(M).
\]

Finally, a nonzero map \(M\to\mathbf F_2\) would have kernel \(T_0\), identifying the nontrivial head \(V\) with the trivial module \(\mathbf F_2\). Hence

\[
(M^\vee)^C=0.
\]

Any elementary boundary decoration kills \(R=\Phi(K)\) and then factors on \(K\) through a \(C\)-map \(M\to E\). Equation (157.4) makes that map zero. Thus

\[
K\leq\ker q_Y,
\]

so quotienting by \(K\) or \(R\), taking scalar pushouts, and performing shears all preserve the fixed boundary exactly.

## 3. The four recurrence branches are exhaustive and rank-halving

Since \(R\) is elementary abelian, exactly four cases occur.

| Branch | Largest possible child marked kernel |
|---|---:|
| \(R=1\) | \(\leq |L|/2\) |
| \(|R|\geq4\) | \(2|L/R|\leq |L|/2\) |
| \(R\cong C_2\), nonzero radical edge | \(|L/R|=|L|/2\) |
| \(R\cong C_2\), zero radical edge | \(\leq |L|/2\), with auxiliary covers actually \(\leq |L|/4\) |

For the last bound, \(V\) is nontrivial over \(\mathbf F_2\), so \(\dim V\geq2\). Hence

\[
|M|\geq|V|\geq4,\qquad
|K|=|R||M|\geq8.
\]

Thus a central double cover over \(C=Y/K\) has marked kernel at most

\[
2|L/K|\leq |L|/4.
\]

Every proper exact-image child projecting onto the same lower quotient has index at least two, and therefore marked-kernel order at most \(|L|/2\).

This proves directly that the target recurrence DAG has no same-rank edge and has depth at most

\[
\left\lceil\log_2|L|\right\rceil.
\]

It independently confirms the strict-descent assertion built into the frozen certificate schema. 

## Consequence

The terminal/active dichotomy, the active-block existence theorem, boundary invisibility, and every rank-descent inequality are source-independent finite-group statements. They therefore survive the retirement of the old \(A_2\) word and apply unchanged to the frozen \(h_0\) source.

Combined with the independently executable Proposition-I, II, and III certificates, this removes the largest unaudited structural component of the target recurrence. I found no hidden Maschke-normality assumption, no use of \([K,\widetilde N]\) itself being normal, and no equal-rank recursive child.

The private 5402-group verifier has still not been run, so this remains a proof-stack audit rather than a reported verifier pass.

**ESTABLISHED**: Independently proved the complete source-free terminal/active-block dichotomy. Terminal targets are canonically \(H\times_{H_2}Q\), including their exact-image posets. Every nonterminal target admits a minimal active block with \(\Phi(K)\) central elementary, \(K^4=1\), scalar radical \(T_0\), simple nontrivial head \(V\), and no trivial quotient. Verified that all four frozen recurrence branches reduce marked-kernel order by at least a factor of two. The frozen \(h_0\) word and hash remain unchanged.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The full target-recurrence manuscript still lacks genuinely external line-by-line review, and this turn does not independently rederive every numerical coefficient in the four recurrence formulas.

**NEXT**: Build a machine-readable target-node validator for the terminal pullback, active-block conditions, subgroup incidences, and branch-specific rank bounds, so the root certificate covers the target-incidence node as executably as Propositions I–III.
